chunks.text: February 24th, 2026 Senate Press Release by ghogari | Mar 3, 2026 | Latest News, Priorities Published 03/03/2026 Los Angeles, CA – At the February 24th meeting of the USG Senate, the Senate took up 3 bills and one resolution — SB 145-18, SB 145-19, and SB 145-20. The Senate passed SB 145-18: A Bill to Amend the USG 2025-2026 Fiscal Budget by a 12-0 vote. Authored by Chief Financial Officer Avery Horton and Chief Programming Officer Ali Edmonds, SB 145-18 will fulfill the request to amend the previously approved budget in SB 145-12. Spring re-allocation ensures funds are being utilized to their highest potential and ensures that by the end of the 2025-2026 calendar year the budget is optimized with a near-zero delta. SB 145-19: A Resolution to support the implementation of Unit Rollover Program as an equitable replacement for the Academic Achievement Award.

Authored by Senator Muqtadir and Aide Corey Umamoto, SB 145-19 urges the university administration to implement the Unit Rollover Program, a solution that allows up to four units to be rolled over per semester, eight within one academic year. The program would operate on a revenue-neutral basis, leveraging existing systems like registration and STARS for automated tracking, and would support all full-time students, rather than reaching only 3% like the former Academic Achievement Award. Senator Muqtadir stated “Unit rollover is a common sense solution that takes into account USC’s financial hurdles over the past year, but also considers that students should be prioritized and be given every opportunity to maximize the value of the units that they are already paying for.” SB 145-19 passed by a 12-0 vote.

The Senate additionally took up SB 145-20: A Bill to Approve changing the Undocumented Trojans Success Assembly’s Name and Logo. The proposed name and logo are intended to be inclusive of students with asylum status while affirming and supporting the broader immigrant community. The bill to rename the organization has passed, and the name will be updated to Immigrant Trojan Student Assembly (ITSA), with the logo reflecting the new acronym. The February 24th Senate meeting also featured reports from Chief Communications Officer Emily Chang, Chief of Staff Karen Borglund, and Chief Financial Officer Avery Horton. The USG Senate meets every Tuesday at 7:00 p.m. in TCC 450 (Tutor Forum). Every Senate meeting has an open forum. This is a space for any person to express ideas and/or concerns to the Senate in a verbal manner.
Upcoming USG Events UTSA Town Hall Meeting – Thursday, February 26th from 5-6 PM (Location via EngageSC) APASA Common Grounds Panel – Friday, February 27th @ TCC Forum from 7-8:30 PM

Fall 2025 Senate Special Elections Result by ghogari | Oct 13, 2025 | Chatbot, Latest News, Priorities Published 10/02/2025 Los Angeles, CA – At the September 30th meeting of the USG Senate, Chief Justice Ryan Tung announced the winner of the Fall 2025 Senate Special Elections. Senator Zachary Garcia, a senior studying political science, was elected as an Undergraduate Student Government senator after running on a “Zack on Track” campaign shown on his campaign platform via Instagram. The Senate Special Elections occurred upon the resignation of Senator Dakota Driemeyer, and now Senator Zachary Garcia will fill this once vacant seat in the 145th Senate class. Garcia ran on a campaign platform where he shared his efforts to stay on track with Zack. “Zack on Track” focuses on providing affordable transportation, partnerships, and maintenance.

“I just want to say a big thank you to the Trojan family for all the support! After hundreds of conversations with students during my campaign and hearing so many incredible stories, I know our university is a community made up of leaders and advocates,” shared Senator Zachary Garcia. “Following that same spirit, as senator, I promise to be a resource for everyone, making sure our USC experience is one worth remembering!” At the September 30th meeting of the USG Senate, Chief Justice Ryan Tung officially inaugurated Senator Zachary Garcia, who will serve for the remainder of the legislative USG year. The USG Senate meets every Tuesday at 7:00 p.m. in TCC 450 (Tutor Forum). Every Senate meeting has an open forum. This is a space for any person to express ideas and/or concerns to the Senate in a verbal manner.

Resources See an exhaustive list of resources within and outside USG. Learn More AboutUSG Information Emergency Contacts Advocacy and Academic Support Financial Support Health Recreation Student Organizations (RSOs) Branches and DepartmentsExecutive Cabinet Executive Cabinet Aides Programming Department Communications Department Funding Department Judicial Branch Legislative Branch Advocacy Liaisons Committees Health and Wellness HIV Testing RSVP Services Student Health Menstrual Product Map Counseling & Mental Health  USC Student Mental Health & Counseling Visit USC Student Health to learn more about programs like individual and group therapy, crisis support, psychiatric services and specialties for gender-based harm. v Crisis Text Line Crisis Text Line is a free service that connects individuals in any type of crisis to a trained Crisis Counselor.

Visit their website for more information and resources.  988 Lifeline The 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline is a national network of local crisis centers that provides free and confidential emotional support to people in suicidal crisis or emotional distress 24 hours a day, 7 days a week in the United States. Updated 03/05/26

Executive Branch The faces that represent you What We Do The USG Executive Branch is the overseeing body of the Undergraduate Student Government. It is comprised of the elected President, Mikaela Bautista, Vice President, Emma Fallon, their Chief of Staff, Karen Borglund, and the Executive Cabinet that oversees the functions of the different departments within USG. The Executive Branch also contains the USG Communications, Programming and Funding departments. MEET OUR TEAM OUR PLATFORM Committed to making progress on the M+E platform while communicating clear, transparent updates to the student body, our administration has been focused on: starting continuing concluding Academic Advising Met with Dean Bullock of Dornsife to touch base on the current state of Dornsife Advising and discuss initial concerns and questions regarding the new Cluster Advising setup.

For the rest of the semester, we will meet monthly with the Dean to address student concerns and help streamline resources for the student body. Consistently keeping tabs on status of academic advisors throughout the layoffs. Free ChatGPT USC has officially announced that it will be partnering with ChatGPT EDU. While we were not directly involved in the final decision to launch ChatGPT EDU, this initiative fulfills our original goal of making a large language model more accessible and available campus-wide, as outlined in our platform. We were honored to participate in subcommittees, meet with administration, and speak with President Kim to share students’ thoughts and concerns throughout the process. Hosted Dr.Helena Sei from the school of Rossier to discuss how to ethically use AI Hosted an AI roundtable to gather feedback from students surrounding AI in the classroom.

Responses will be given to the Center for Excellence in teaching to inform their practices when working with faculty. We will continue to meet with the administration and President Kim to ensure that student perspective is included in decisions and implementation. Discount Lyft- USC Shuttle Senator Murthy is continuing conversations regarding Waymo discounts while also advocating for the extension of Lyft hours. Senator Murthy has sent out a survey for the extension of Lyft hours. Campus Activities Successfully managed a budget to ensure CA has the capacity to process budgets. Created our new RSO Liaison position to help communicate with RSOs about the application process and our opportunities Working closely with Campus Activities to workshop the position Extended Doheny Hours Met with Dr.Palascios to discuss feasibility and proposal of this during the next budget meeting.

Receiving contact to organize a meeting! Menstrual Product Access Successfully established a Period Pantry located in the Office of Basic Needs. Senator Murthy & DS team implemented a digital map of campus that shows where menstrual products are available. Local School Initiatives Field day! External Affairs Chair Juni and Academic Affairs Chair Nguyen heading the project and picking up communication from VP Fallon and Chief of Staff. First Generation Student Assembly Mentorship program with partnership schools External Affairs Chair Juni and FGSA Co-Execs are working to create a student mentor ship program between USC and First-gen students Mobile ID Cards Confirmation that ID cards will be implemented in 3+ years on the current schedule. Senator Shih and President Bautista are continuing to meet with the administration to ensure the implementation process runs smoothly.

Study Spaces Based on previous advocacy from the Academic Affairs Committee regarding the lack of late study spaces on campus, Student Life has decided to open all rooms in the STU, TCC, and SKS Center for students to study. Rooms are open until 11 pm. Trojan Family room is being rebranded to be an interactive study space with more seating, music, and games. Continuing conversations surrounding later hours during finals week. Senator Murthy working on Leavey Basement Advocacy for extended Doheny hours Welcome Dinner Officially in the planning process with the Welcome Back team for our welcome dinner for spring admits, transfer, and newly admitted students. USG Culture USG Families Created a point system created to encourage collaboration, connections, and provide a space for people to seek community within USG aside from working.

Facilitating a vibrant and friendly office environment by prioritizing team meetings, interdepartmental collaboration, and day-to-day bonding. Updated ORCA (our HR system) guidelines. USG Transparency Recording and broadcasting Senate meetings. Improving communication in connection with the student body- updating our website, senators tabling, and hosting an open house and info session about USG. Connecting with more student groups to share forms, receive feedback, and bridge the gap in the student body RSOs Panhellenic Media Implementing an open house for students to come see the USG office Football Games With the cancellation of the Spring 2026 football game meeting, we connected with Trojan Pride to begin laying the foundation for increasing school spirit next year through themed games (e.g., blackout, whiteout, etc.).

Senior Events Collaborating with the Alumni Association to enhance Grad Week celebrations for graduating seniors. Will be meeting soon to discuss ideas and current initiatives. RSO Recognition Assistance RSO Liaison Created RSO Liaison, a new position in USG, to be in charge of assisting RSOs and students with the re-recognition process, answering questions and staying in contact with RSOs to make them aware of resources, events, and gathering feedback. Advocating for RSOs in all our meetings and offering any help to campus activities with administrative burden. Who We Are Executive Cabinet 2025-26 President Mikaela Bautista Vice President Emma Fallon Chief of Staff Karen Borglund Chief Financial Officer Avery Horton Chief Communications Officer Emily Chang Chief Programming Officer Ali Edmonds Chief Diversity Officer Yasmeen ElFarra Speaker of the Senate Jeremiah Boisrund

Programming Find your place on campus What We Do The Undergraduate Student Government’s Programming Department works formally with student organizations on campus to coordinate activities and pool resources. It is made up of special interest committees and community-focused assemblies with the common goal of promoting diversity, education, and entertainment through on-campus events. Programming Assemblies '25-26 The Programming Branch’s 16 community-focused assemblies focus on representing the interests of students from a wide variety of backgrounds. They act as umbrella organizations for numerous member organizations, each playing an important role in event planning and sharing information at assembly meetings. Assemblies also serve as a source of funding and advisement for their member organizations.

Academic Culture Assembly The Academic Culture Assembly encourages the pursuit of learning through interdisciplinary exploration and programming. ACA strives to remind students that learning is fun by fostering an inclusive environment that promotes curiosity and conversations beyond the classroom. ACA is an umbrella organization for academic student organizations on campus, providing a community of support, guidance, and funding for member organizations, who are encouraged to collaborate on events. Follow us on Instagram! Contact: usgaca@usc.edu Asian Pacific American Student Assembly The Asian Pacific American Student Assembly works to celebrate and share Asian Pacific Islander Desi American (APIDA) heritage and diversity, USC APASA supports its member organizations and sponsors cultural events to foster unity and growth within and beyond the APIDA Trojan community.

We strive to create a more united community, where students support each other culturally, socially, and academically by educating each other about their heritage, history, and traditions. Follow us on Instagram! Contact: apasa@usc.edu Black Student Assembly The USC Black Student Assembly develops and strengthens leaders, services their community, and provides infrastructure that fosters camaraderie amongst students of the Black culture. BSA strives toward the advancement of those of African descent and emphasizes the importance of educating the USC and neighboring community about their shared struggles and successes. Follow us on Instagram! Contact: usgbsa@usc.edu Environmental Student Assembly The Environmental Student Assembly is dedicated to programming events promoting the green culture, sustainability, and environmental advocacy at USC.

Members develop both environmental and sustainable initiatives for the USC community. ESA also engages with the local LA community to bring awareness to these global issues. Follow us on Instagram! Contact: uscesa@usc.edu First Generation Student Assembly The First Generation Student Assembly (FGSA) is dedicated to supporting first generation college students across USC. Follow us on Instagram! Contact: uscfgsu@gmail.com International Student Assembly The International Student Assembly (ISA) serves as an umbrella organization to a majority of the nationality clubs on campus, working closely with the Undergraduate Student Government, member organizations, and the Office of International Services, to actively promote the international community on campus. ISA’s mission is to promote international culture and raise diversity awareness on campus. Follow us on Instagram!

Contact: usgisa@usc.edu Joint Assembly for Military-Associated Students The Joint Assembly for Military-Associated Students is USC’s community for Military-Associated students. Follow us on Instagram! Contact: usgjams@usc.edu Latine Student Assembly The primary focus of the Latine Student Assembly is to foster pride in their incredibly diverse and nuanced culture by bringing together students of the heritage, in order to amplify and support the needs of the Latine student population. In addition, LSA continues to educated the larger student body and are committed to reimagining the role that USC plays in its surrounding community. Follow us on Instagram! Contact: usglsa@usc.edu Native American Student Assembly The Native American Student Assembly is a non-profit umbrella group for Native American/American Indian student organizations at USC.

As the only Native American programming entity on campus, our mission is to voice the concerns and needs of the Native American student body at USC, as well as provide Native American, American Indian, and non-native students the opportunity to connect and empower one another. Follow us on Instagram! Contact: uscnasa@usc.edu Middle Eastern North African Student Assembly The Middle Eastern North African Student Assembly organizes and facilitates spaces for MENA-identifying students and all those interested to express themselves culturally, create connections, and give back to their communities. As the focal umbrella organization for the diverse MENA cultural clubs on campus, MENASA emphasizes unity and raising awareness about the MENA region and diaspora, often misrepresented in the media and misunderstood in Western society. Follow us on Instagram!

Contact: uscmenasa@gmail.com Political Student Assembly The Political Student Assembly works to foster diverse and healthy political discourse and political activism among students at USC, by promoting political awareness and discussions on campus. We host a series of debate and discussion events, bring in speakers of all political stripes, and support and partner with a wide array of political organizations on campus. Follow us on Instagram! Contact: uscpsa@usc.edu Queer and Ally Student Assembly The Queer and Ally Student Assembly provides social, educational, and cultural events for Queer students to engage with and participate in. We foster the LGBTQ+ community on campus through events like the annual Drag Show, Second Chance Prom, National Coming Out Month, and leadership retreats.

We also bring speakers and other community partners to campus to cultivate an engaging and dynamic community on campus. Follow us on Instagram! Contact: quasa@usc.edu Student Assembly for Accessibility The mission of the Student Assembly for Accessibility is to empower and represent students with a wide variety of disabilities. SAA seeks to create a space dedicated to promoting cultural awareness of diverse experiences within the disabled community, addressing accessibility concerns, and facilitating solidarity to establish a platform for the voices of students with disabilities to be heard. Follow us on Instagram! Contact: usc.accessibility@gmail.com Student Assembly for Gender Empowerment The mission of the Student Assembly for Gender Empowerment is to program events to address emerging feminist concerns of students, faculty, and staff.

SAGE seeks to uplift all voices oppressed by the patriarchy in dynamic, critical ways. We are a queer and trans inclusive organization working towards intersectional, collective liberation. Follow us on Instagram! Contact: uscsage@usc.edu Transfer Student Assembly The mission of the Transfer Student Assembly (TSA) is to create a platform to advocate for and raise awareness of transfer issues on campus and assist students and administration to bridge the gap between transfer students and the greater USC community. TSA seeks to build a sense of connection to the Trojan Family, a supportive community, and provide resources for all students who might face challenges in successfully transitioning on campus to build a shared foundation for academic, social, and professional growth. Follow us on Instagram!

s Contact: tsausc@usc.edu Immigrant Trojan Student Assembly The Immigrant Trojan Student Assembly is dedicated to creating a safe and supportive space for undocumented+ students and allies at USC. Our mission is to raise awareness, advocate for the immigrant community on campus, and foster a strong sense of belonging through social events. Undocumented+ refers to non-citizen individuals including undocumented, DACAmented, TPS, refugees, asylum seekers, U-visa recipients, and individuals pursuing legalization. Follow us on Instagram! Contact: ideasusc@usc.edu Last Updated 02/26/26 Programming Committees '25-26 The 4 special-interest committees focused on concerts, performing arts, guest speakers, and school spirit. If you can dream it, a committee can plan it! Concerts Committee The Concerts Committee organizes concerts and programs to bring the hottest bands and musicians to campus!

We also host individuals from the world of entertainment for our speaker series, providing our committee members with more insight to what it really means to work and thrive in the music industry. Anyone with an interest in live music is encouraged to come to our meetings on Mondays at 9:00 pm in TCC 450. Follow us on Instagram! Contact: sconcert@usc.edu Performing Arts Committee Performing Arts Committee strives to create innovative opportunities for creative students interested in visual and performing arts. From fostering collaboration between USC artists, to bringing in outside performers, Performing Arts Committee immerses USC students in many different art forms, ranging from dance, music, theater, painting, photography, and so much more. If you are interested in any form of art, come to our meetings to see what more you can do with the arts community here at USC!

Follow us on Instagram! Contact: usgpac@usc.edu Trojan Pride Committee Trojan Pride is the quintessential spirit group for any USC Trojan fan. Coordinating with the athletic department to increase visibility and attendance at USC athletic events, this organization is home to the loudest, most dedicated fans. From front row seats at football and basketball games to away games across the state, the most avid Trojan fans can definitely be found in Trojan Pride. Follow us on Instagram! Contact: scpride@usc.edu Speakers Committee The Speakers Committee serves the USC community by bringing a range of speakers to campus every year to provide insight, education, entertainment, and the promotion of diversity through cultural awareness. Past Speakers include Barack Obama, Maya Angelou, Stan Lee, and Angela Davis. All are welcome to join! Follow us on Instagram! Contact: speakers@usc.edu
